data.title: Alternate Contact Method Offered for VEYYO
data.description: Blockchain sleuth ZachXBT posted on X, offering an alternate contact method for VEYYO. He addressed @SWAPD_com, @circle, @FastCompany, and @jerallaire, requesting them to send a direct message as their current contact methods are closed.

data.title: Germany's Central Bank: Economic Stagnation Likely in First Quarter
data.description: Germany's central bank has indicated that the country's economy may experience stagnation in the first quarter. According to Jin10, this forecast comes amid ongoing challenges in the global economic landscape. The bank's assessment highlights concerns over potential impacts on growth, driven by various external and internal factors. As Germany navigates these economic hurdles, policymakers are closely monitoring developments to mitigate adverse effects and support recovery efforts.

data.title: Germany's Central Bank: Inflation Rate Expected to Rise to 3% in Short Term
data.description: Germany's central bank has projected that the country's inflation rate will increase to approximately 3% in the near future. According to Jin10, this forecast comes amid ongoing economic challenges and global market fluctuations. The central bank's assessment highlights concerns over rising prices and their potential impact on the economy. This development is part of a broader trend of inflationary pressures being observed in various economies worldwide. The bank's outlook suggests that policymakers may need to consider measures to address these inflationary trends to maintain economic stability.

data.title: Former Fund Manager Justin Leverenz Returns with New Strategy
data.description: Justin Leverenz, who previously managed the largest emerging-markets mutual fund, is making a comeback with a new approach after facing significant losses and outflows. Bloomberg posted on X that Leverenz's career at Invesco concluded following a pivotal decision. His return marks a shift in strategy, aiming to navigate the challenges of the financial landscape with innovative methods.

data.title: Vertiv Stock Reaches Record High Amid Strong Year-to-Date Gains
data.description: Vertiv's stock recently achieved an all-time high of $282, closing at $276, marking a nearly 58% increase year-to-date. According to NS3.AI, this performance highlights the company's strong market position. Long-term investors might consider employing a dollar-cost averaging strategy, while more conservative investors may prefer to wait for a potential pullback before making any investment decisions.

data.title: President Macron to Address Paris Blockchain Week in April
data.description: President Emmanuel Macron is set to deliver a special address at the Paris Blockchain Week, scheduled for April 15–16 at the Carrousel du Louvre. According to NS3.AI, this appearance is considered a significant milestone for the conference. The event will feature various attending institutions and provide details on the venue and exhibitor access.

data.title: Coca-Cola HBC Secures €1.5 Billion Debt for Acquisition
data.description: Coca-Cola HBC is set to raise a minimum of €1.5 billion ($1.7 billion) in debt to finance the acquisition of another beverage bottling company. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic move aimed at expanding Coca-Cola HBC's market presence. The acquisition is part of the company's broader strategy to enhance its operational capabilities and market reach in the competitive beverage industry. This financial maneuver underscores Coca-Cola HBC's commitment to growth and consolidation within the sector.

data.title: Iran Denies Negotiations as Trump Signals Deal Push, Oil Rises and Risk Sentiment Weakens
data.description: Key TakeawaysDonald Trump claims Iran is seeking a deal, while Tehran denies active negotiations.Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi says only indirect messages have been exchanged.Ceasefire uncertainty is pushing oil prices higher and weakening global risk sentiment.Ongoing conflict continues to impact inflation expectations and crypto market outlook.Conflicting signals between the United States and Iran over a potential ceasefire are adding to geopolitical uncertainty, with implications for global markets including crypto.U.S. President Donald Trump said Iranian leaders are seeking a deal to end nearly four weeks of conflict, describing them as eager but hesitant to publicly engage. However, Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi rejected the claim, stating that no negotiations are currently underway.Araqchi confirmed that Iran is reviewing a U.S. proposal, but emphasized that communications have only occurred indirectly through intermediaries and do not constitute formal talks.The lack of clarity around a ceasefire has begun to weigh on global markets. Initial optimism faded, with equities losing momentum and oil prices resuming their climb amid concerns over supply disruptions.The conflict’s economic impact is expanding, with fuel shortages and logistical disruptions affecting businesses globally. Rising energy costs are reinforcing inflation risks, complicating the macro outlook.For crypto markets, the development adds another layer of uncertainty. Higher oil prices and persistent geopolitical tension tend to reduce risk appetite in the short term, which can pressure Bitcoin and other digital assets.

data.title: Trump: Iran should take this seriously soon, lest it be "too late".
data.description: Trump: Iran should take this seriously soon, lest it be "too late." (Jinshi)

data.title: Therium Faces Lawsuit Over Alleged Role in Malaysia Arbitration Case
data.description:
Litigation funder Therium is currently embroiled in a legal battle over accusations of its involvement in a conspiracy to secure a $14.9 billion arbitration award from Malaysia. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the allegations that Therium played a significant part in the cross-continent scheme.

The lawsuit claims that Therium was instrumental in orchestrating the arbitration process, which has drawn significant attention due to the substantial amount involved. The case underscores the complexities and controversies surrounding international arbitration and the role of litigation funders in such high-stakes legal proceedings.

Therium has yet to publicly respond to the allegations, and the outcome of the lawsuit could have far-reaching implications for the litigation funding industry. The case continues to unfold, with legal experts closely monitoring its developments.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump: Iranian Negotiators Are 'Strange' and 'Begging' for a Deal
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has described Iranian negotiators as 'very different' and 'strange,' asserting that they are 'begging' for an agreement. According to Jin10, Trump stated that this behavior is expected as Iran's military capabilities have been completely destroyed, leaving them with no chance of recovery. The comments come amid ongoing tensions between the United States and Iran, with diplomatic efforts to reach a resolution continuing.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Warns of Consequences if Negotiation Window Closes
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has issued a warning regarding the potential consequences if the opportunity for negotiations is missed. According to Jin10, Trump emphasized that once the window for talks closes, there will be no turning back, and the outcomes could be unfavorable. This statement underscores the urgency and importance he places on timely negotiations in the current geopolitical climate. The President's remarks highlight the critical nature of ongoing discussions and the potential ramifications of failing to reach an agreement.

data.title: White House Clears Rule That Could Open $10 Trillion 401(k) Market to Bitcoin and Crypto
data.description: Key TakeawaysWhite House review cleared a Labor Department rule that could allow crypto in $10T 401(k) retirement market.The proposal would amend fiduciary guidance under ERISA to include Bitcoin and alternative assets.Rule classified as “economically significant,” signaling major potential market impact.Move follows Trump executive order pushing broader access to digital assets in retirement plans.The White House has cleared a key regulatory review of a proposed rule that could open the $10 trillion U.S. 401(k) market to Bitcoin and other digital assets, marking a significant step toward mainstream crypto adoption in retirement portfolios.The Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs (OIRA), part of the Executive Office of Donald Trump, completed its review on March 24, allowing the Department of Labor to proceed with formally publishing the proposal.If finalized, the rule would amend fiduciary guidance for retirement plans governed by the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA), potentially permitting plan sponsors to include cryptocurrency and private equity as investment options within participant-directed 401(k) plans.The proposal follows a prior executive order from Trump directing federal agencies, including the Securities and Exchange Commission and Treasury Department, to explore expanding access to alternative assets—such as crypto—within retirement accounts.OIRA classified the rule as “economically significant,” indicating it could have a material impact on the U.S. economy, typically defined as exceeding $200 million annually.The development comes as retirement savings continue to grow. Data from Fidelity Investments shows the average 401(k) balance reached $144,400 in Q3 2025, up 9% year-over-year, while IRA balances rose to $137,902.If implemented, the rule could significantly expand institutional demand for Bitcoin by enabling retirement funds to allocate capital into crypto markets, potentially reshaping long-term inflows and market structure.

data.title: Bank of England Deputy Governor: UK Bond Market Stable, No Evidence of Forced Selling
data.description: The Deputy Governor of the Bank of England, Briden, has stated that the UK government bond market has been operating normally, with no signs of forced selling. According to Jin10, Briden emphasized the stability of the market, dismissing concerns about any abnormal activities or pressures that might lead to forced sales. The statement comes amid ongoing scrutiny of global financial markets, where volatility and economic uncertainties have raised questions about the resilience of various sectors. Briden's remarks aim to reassure investors and stakeholders about the health of the UK's bond market, highlighting its ability to function smoothly despite external challenges.

data.title: Traders Consider Impact of Trump's April Tariff Reversal
data.description: Traders are currently evaluating the implications of U.S. President Donald Trump's decision in April to reverse tariffs, as they consider whether to sell. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing deliberations among market participants. The tariff reversal has introduced uncertainty into the market, prompting traders to reassess their strategies in light of potential economic shifts. This decision by the Trump administration is seen as a significant factor influencing trading activities and market dynamics.

data.title: Crypto Stocks Fall Pre-Market as Coinbase, MicroStrategy, Circle Decline
data.description: Key TakeawaysCrypto-related stocks trade lower pre-market, led by Circle (CRCL) down 2.3%.MicroStrategy (MSTR) falls 1.66%, while Coinbase (COIN) drops 1.88%.Bitcoin miners and related equities also decline, reflecting weaker risk sentiment.Broader move signals cautious positioning ahead of market open.Crypto-related stocks in the U.S. are trading broadly lower in pre-market hours, tracking cautious sentiment across digital asset markets.Circle (CRCL) declined 2.3%, leading losses among crypto-linked equities. Other notable movers include ALTS (-1.48%), UPXI (-3.36%), BMNR (-2.45%), and SBET (-2.34%).Major crypto proxies also edged lower. Shares of MicroStrategy fell 1.66%, while Coinbase dropped 1.88%. Bitcoin mining stocks also weakened, with MARA down 2.17%.The declines come amid subdued momentum in Bitcoin and broader risk assets, as macro uncertainty and geopolitical developments continue to weigh on sentiment.Data cited from msx.com, a decentralized real-world asset (RWA) trading platform offering tokenized exposure to U.S. equities and ETFs, indicates the pullback is widespread across crypto-linked names.The pre-market weakness suggests investors remain cautious ahead of the U.S. trading session, with crypto equities continuing to act as a high-beta proxy for Bitcoin price movements and overall market risk appetite.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Criticizes NATO's Inaction on Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed criticism towards NATO countries for their lack of action regarding Iran, which he described as a 'crazy country' that has been militarily destroyed. According to Jin10, Trump emphasized that the United States does not require any assistance from NATO but urged to 'never forget' this significant moment.

data.title: Startale Group completes $63 million Series A funding round, with SBI and Sony participating.
data.description: Startale Group has completed a $63 million Series A funding round, with SBI Group investing $50 million and Sony Innovation Fund investing $13 million. This round of funding will be used to advance its on-chain infrastructure development, including Layer 2 networks, stablecoins, and tokenized securities. Startale stated that the funds will also be used to expand into Asian on-chain capital markets and promote the deployment of related products.

data.title: Bank of England Deputy Governor: Benchmark Interest Rate Remains Tight as Labor Slack Increases
data.description: Bank of England Deputy Governor, Ben Broadbent, has stated that the current benchmark interest rate is maintaining a tight stance amid increasing labor market slack. According to Jin10, Broadbent emphasized the importance of monitoring economic indicators closely to assess the impact of monetary policy on the labor market. He noted that while the interest rate is designed to curb inflation, the growing slack in the labor force could pose challenges to achieving economic stability. Broadbent's comments come as the Bank of England continues to navigate complex economic conditions, balancing inflation control with the need to support employment. The Deputy Governor's remarks highlight the ongoing debate within the central bank regarding the appropriate level of interest rates to foster sustainable economic growth.

data.title: Germany Considers Further Measures to Address Rising Fuel Costs
data.description: German Economy Minister Katherina Reiche announced that the coalition government is contemplating additional strategies to mitigate the effects of escalating fuel prices. Bloomberg posted on X that this consideration follows the approval of an initial package by lawmakers aimed at addressing the issue. The government is actively exploring options to provide relief to consumers and businesses affected by the rising costs. The initial measures are part of a broader effort to stabilize the economy amid fluctuating energy prices.
